messages.text: Played guitar in the plaza tonight. Old couple stopped to dance. These are the moments that make the city real.
They asked me to move from my usual corner. 'Noise optimization.' I found another spot. It's fine. Probably.
Fewer people stop now. They walk faster. Eyes forward. My tip jar's been empty two days running.
Haven't played in a week. It's not that I'm forbidden. It just... doesn't feel right anymore. Like the quiet wants me to match it.
Sold my guitar. No one was listening anyway.
Best morning rush in months! Chai is flowing, regulars are arguing about football. This is what a city sounds like.
New quiet hours mean I close earlier. Lost the late-night study crowd. Budget's tighter this month.
Most of my tables are empty now. People don't linger. They order, drink, leave. No one stays to talk.
Got my 'non-productive space assessment' today. Apparently cafes don't contribute to efficiency metrics. Who writes this?
Closing the cafe. Not because I was told to. Because there's no one left to serve.
[CITIZEN RELOCATED — NO FORWARDING ADDRESS]
Kids wrote poems about their neighborhood today. Amir's was about his grandfather's bakery. Made me cry.
New curriculum guidelines arrived. 'Creative subjects reallocated to technical skills.' I'm keeping poetry Fridays anyway.
Poetry Fridays got flagged in my performance review. 'Non-aligned with efficiency standards.' Since when is wonder inefficient?
The children don't ask 'why' anymore. They ask 'what's the correct answer.' I taught them that. I'm part of this.
Amir's grandfather's bakery closed last month. Amir wrote a report about it. Factual. Efficient. No feeling at all.
[EMPLOYMENT STATUS: AUTOMATED]
Quiet shift tonight. Couple having a picnic on the roof. Pretended I didn't see them. Some rules are meant to be ignored.
They installed sensors in my sector. The system sees everything now. Doesn't matter what I pretend not to notice.
Filed 23 anomaly reports this week. Mostly people walking too slowly or standing too long in one place. Is that a crime now?
I'm not security anymore. I'm a compliance officer. Different badge. Same hallways. But now I'm the thing people are afraid of.
The rooftop couple from before. Saw their names on a deviation report today. I flagged them. I flagged them and I didn't even hesitate.
[STATUS: COMPLIANT]
